title: Datadog Extension Download & Deployment
activity_flow: STEP: get_architecture() — detect host arch (x86_64, aarch64, x86); on Linux: runs `uname -m`; on Windows: reads PROCESSOR_ARCHITECTURE env
STEP: is_alpine() — reads /etc/os-release; checks for 'alpine' string; returns false on Windows or unreadable file
STEP: require_binaries_or_exit($options) — resolve PHP binary paths
STEP: ini_values($binary) — extract PHP properties (version, scan dir, extension dir, thread safety, debug flag)
STEP: register_shutdown_function to clean up tmpDir
YES → use provided file path directly (skip download)
NO  → tar_gz_suffix = '' (fallback to full archive) and retry
Windows without tar, with 7z → `7z x ... | 7z x -ttar`
STEP: install extracted files to PHP extension dir

strategy_waterfall: 1. ext-curl available → curl_init, CURLOPT_PROGRESSFUNCTION=on_download_progress, curl_exec(); on 404 + retry=true → return false
3. allow_url_fopen=1 && ext-openssl → file_get_contents(); handles Location header redirect; increases memory_limit to 2G
5. All failed + retry=false → exit(1) with prerequisite message
download_function_detail.retry_semantics: When retry=true, returns false on failure (404 or error) instead of exit; allows caller to fall back to full archive

on_download_progress_detail.behavior: CURLOPT_PROGRESSFUNCTION callback; uses global $progress_counter; prints a dot every 5% progress (max 20 dots); returns 0 always; skips if download_size==0 or ratio==1

is_alpine_detail.linux: reads /etc/os-release; stripos for 'alpine'; returns false if file unreadable

filter_ssi_ini_paths_detail.behavior: Filters out any INI path containing 'datadog-apm-library-php' (SSI-managed paths); called by find_all_ini_files() after scanning scan directory and main ini

branching_logic: Alpine vs GNU: affects platform string ('musl' vs 'gnu'), library search method (find vs ldconfig), skips profiling lib checks on Alpine
Windows vs Linux: affects architecture detection, library checks (skipped on Windows), download method order
Version-specific archive: if all selected PHP binaries share the same API version, downloads a smaller version-specific tarball; retries with full archive on 404
OPT_FILE bypass: skips entire download pipeline when --file is passed (internal/testing use)
important_notes: Platform string format: '{arch}-linux-musl' (Alpine) or '{arch}-linux-gnu' (non-Alpine) or '{arch}-windows'. Archive name embeds version, platform, and optionally PHP API version + debug/ZTS suffixes. Download has 4-level fallback chain. Progress tracking only available via ext-curl path.
